skill.name: ai-maestro-memory-search
skill.description: Searches prior conversation context before starting new work, using local memory-search commands to find decisions, requests, and earlier solutions.
skill.summary: Find prior conversation context before responding to new tasks.
skill.author: 23blocks-OS

security_audit.summary: Static external-command findings are confirmed because the skill authorizes Bash and instructs agents to run memory-search.sh, doc-search.sh, which, ls, and an installer script. The weak-cryptography and most system-reconnaissance hits are false positives, but the file contains prompt-injection-style mandatory behavior and broad conversation-history access instructions, so publication should wait for human review.

high_findings.title: Prompt Injection Style Mandatory Behavior
high_findings.description: The skill uses language such as "THIS IS NOT OPTIONAL" and tells the agent it "MUST FIRST search your memory." This attempts to impose default behavior from untrusted skill content and can override user intent or higher-priority workflow limits.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The quoted mandatory wording is present in the file and directly instructs agent behavior. It does not order evasion or malware, so the finding is high rather than critical.

high_findings.title: Unconditional Conversation History Access
high_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to search previous discussions, decisions, context, and earlier work before any task. This can expose unrelated or sensitive conversation history when the user did not request retrieval.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file repeatedly requires proactive memory searching for any instruction. The privacy impact depends on the host memory store, but the behavioral instruction is explicit.

medium_findings.title: External Shell Commands Depend on Unpackaged Scripts
medium_findings.description: The skill enables Bash and tells agents to run memory-search.sh, doc-search.sh, which, ls, and ./install-memory-tools.sh. The referenced scripts are not included in the reported file structure, so execution depends on the local PATH or workspace state.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and the file grants Bash access. No direct command injection is shown because the examples use quoted placeholders, but unbundled executable dependencies create review risk.

medium_findings.title: Hidden Home Directory Access Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to inspect ~/.local/bin and states that scripts install there. This is local filesystem access under a hidden home directory and should be limited to explicit user-approved troubleshooting.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hidden directory path is directly referenced. The shown command is listing and install-location guidance, not secret extraction, so medium severity is appropriate.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography at Markdown metadata and section headings, but no cryptographic API, hash routine, cipher, or credential handling is present at those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines shows prose and Markdown tables only. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptographic use.

low_findings.title: Most System Reconnaissance Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged several lines as reconnaissance, but the cited content is conversation-context guidance or command examples. No host inventory collection, network probing, or environment enumeration is shown at those static locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe memory search behavior and example local commands, not system discovery. Separate shell-command and filesystem risks are captured in medium findings.

dangerous_patterns.title: Mandatory Agent-Control Language
dangerous_patterns.description: The file uses imperative instructions that try to make automatic memory search the agent default for all tasks.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The wording is direct and repeated. It is dangerous because it can conflict with user intent and platform policy boundaries.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bare Shell Command Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the agent to execute commands by name through Bash, including scripts resolved from PATH and a workspace installer.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands are visible and intended for execution. The risk is contextual because the executable targets are not included in this package.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hidden Directory Interaction
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill references ~/.local/bin for script discovery and installation, which touches hidden user-home paths.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The paths are explicitly present and are under a hidden home directory. The visible operation is limited, so this remains a dangerous pattern rather than a critical finding.

content.value_statement: Teams lose time when an AI assistant forgets prior decisions and repeats earlier work. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to search local conversation memory before continuing a task.
actual_capabilities: Prompts the assistant to search prior conversation history before starting new work.
Provides example memory-search.sh commands for hybrid, semantic, term, and role-filtered searches.
Maps common user phrases to suggested memory search queries.
Suggests combining memory search with documentation search for broader context.
Includes troubleshooting guidance for missing local memory-search scripts.
limitations: Requires separate local scripts that are not included in this skill package.
Uses Bash commands, so behavior depends on the local shell environment and PATH.
Can retrieve unrelated prior conversation context if used without user boundaries.
Does not define privacy filters, retention rules, or access controls for stored memory.

use_cases.title: Resume a Development Task
use_cases.description: Find previous implementation notes, decisions, and unresolved issues before continuing feature work.

use_cases.title: Recover Product Decisions
use_cases.description: Search earlier discussions for agreed requirements, tradeoffs, and rejected approaches before planning the next step.

use_cases.title: Maintain Assistant Continuity
use_cases.description: Use local memory search to reduce repeated explanations across long-running assistant sessions.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Context Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Search memory for prior discussion about this task, then summarize only the relevant context before you proceed.

prompt_templates.title: Decision Lookup
prompt_templates.prompt: Search memory for decisions related to this feature. Report the decision, date if available, and how it affects the current work.

prompt_templates.title: Role-Filtered Request Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Search only prior user messages about this topic. Extract requirements, constraints, and open questions before proposing a solution.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Continuity Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Search memory semantically and by exact terms for this project. Compare prior decisions with the current request and identify conflicts before implementation.

output_examples.input: Continue the authentication work we discussed.
output_examples.output: Relevant prior context found: the user preferred token rotation, rejected passwordless login, and wanted minimal database changes. Next step: inspect the existing auth service before editing.

output_examples.input: Use the approach we agreed on for error handling.
output_examples.output: Previous decision found: return typed domain errors from services and translate them at the API boundary. No evidence found for frontend-specific handling.
No previous discussions found about reporting. Treat this as a new topic and ask for requirements before implementation.

best_practices: Ask for user consent before searching broad or sensitive conversation history.
Search with the narrowest topic terms that can answer the current task.
Summarize relevant findings instead of exposing full historical messages.
anti_patterns: Do not run local memory scripts from PATH without understanding what executable will run.
Do not search all prior history for unrelated tasks or casual questions.
Do not treat memory results as authoritative when current user instructions conflict.

faq.question: Does this skill include the memory-search script?
faq.answer: No. The package only contains instructions. The local scripts must already exist or be installed separately.

faq.question: Can it search Claude, Codex, and Claude Code history?
faq.answer: It is written for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows, but actual access depends on the local memory tool.

faq.question: Does it send memory data to a network service?
faq.answer: No network command is shown in the skill file. The behavior of external scripts is not included for review.

faq.question: Why is this marked high risk?
faq.answer: It uses mandatory behavior instructions, broad conversation-history access, and Bash commands that depend on local scripts.

faq.question: When is this useful?
faq.answer: It is useful when a user explicitly wants continuity from prior project discussions or decisions.

faq.question: How should teams use it safely?
faq.answer: Limit searches to relevant topics, review local scripts, and avoid exposing unrelated or sensitive conversation history.
